description_long: This is a rounded, bulbous ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a single, thick handle attached near the top. The surface is predominantly off-white with large, irregular dark scorch marks, suggesting it has been exposed to fire or smoke. Around the neck, there is a band of hand-painted, dark brown geometric and dot motifs, giving it a rustic, artisanal appearance. The rim is uneven and slightly misshapen, indicating it may have been hand-formed rather than machine-made. The handle is robust and functional, with a slight curve for easy gripping. The overall finish is matte and rough, with visible texture and some signs of wear or age.
description: A bulbous, off-white ceramic vase with dark scorch marks, a single handle, and hand-painted brown geometric motifs around the neck. The surface is matte and textured, with visible signs of wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main handle, the uneven rim, and the most prominent dark scorch mark on the lower left side.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the back of the handle and a less pronounced scorch mark, with the painted motifs partially visible.
description_view_2: The handle is fully visible from the side, with the largest darkened area on the lower right and the painted band clearly seen.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back of the vase, with the painted motifs partially visible and the handle mostly hidden.
